evidence.source_title: Ali Akbar Mohtashamipour, Shiite cleric and a founder of Hezbollah, dies at 74 — Washington Post
evidence.description: Mohtashamipour played a pivotal role in the creation of Hezbollah while serving as Iran's ambassador to Syria.

evidence.source_title: An Iranian figure who had a key role in founding Hezbollah — Meir Amit Intelligence and Terrorism Information Center
evidence.description: Mohtashami actively supervised Hezbollah's creation, merging existing radical Shia factions into the unified organization.

editorial_notes: Mohtashamipur supervised the merger of Islamic Amal, Lebanese Dawa Party, and other Shia factions into Hezbollah while serving as Iran's ambassador to Syria (1982–1986).
